titlestmt.titleproper: Finding Aid for the John Harris Papers LSC.2392
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Kelly Besser, 2025.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated 2025 October 7.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-03-09 14:39:33 -0700.

physdesc.extent: (21 document boxes and one flat box)

did.abstract: In 1969 poet John Harris founded the Venice Poetry Workshop at Beyond Baroque Literary/Arts Center with Joseph Hansen. Many of the major voices of contemporary poetry in Los Angeles came from that workshop, including Kate Braverman, Wanda Coleman, Bob Flanagan, Michael C. Ford, Lee Hickman, Bill Mohr, Harry Northup and others. Harris operated Papa Bach Paperbacks from 1974-1984, editing the literary journal Bachy and publishing poetry books by Bert Meyers, George Hitchcock and William Pillin. The John Harris papers range from circa 1936 to 2014 and consist of Harris's poetry, personal files, subject files, Papa Bach Books files, and local poetry publications.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. Advance notice is required for access to the collection. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: CONTAINS UNPROCESSED DIGITAL and AUDIO MATERIALS: Digital and audio materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Property rights to the objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], John Harris papers (Collection 2392). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Potentially Offensive Description in Library Special Collections.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: In 1969 John Harris founded the Venice Poetry Workshop at Beyond Baroque Literary/Arts Center with Joseph Hansen. Many of the major voices of contemporary poetry in Los Angeles came from that workshop, including Kate Braverman, Wanda Coleman, Bob Flanagan, Michael C. Ford, Lee Hickman, Bill Mohr, Harry Northup and others. His poems have appeared in dozens of magazines; in the anthologies Holy Doors, New Venice Poets, Poetry Loves Poetry, and Venice Thirteen; and in two collections, Where Love Is and Against The Day Of The Dead. His manuscript Climbing was a finalist for the University of Pittsburgh Press U.S. Poetry Award. In the 70's and 80's he was proprietor of Papa Bach Books, the historic bookstore and literary center where a generation of Los Angeles writers came of age artistically. He published the literary journal Bachy as well as books by George Hitchcock, Bert Meyers and William Pillin.
The "John Harris Bio" was written by Harris and is located in Box 20, Folder 1 of the John Harris papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The John Harris papers range from circa 1936 to 2014. The collection consists of Harris's poetry, personal files, subject files, Papa Bach Books files, and local poetry publications.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged in their existing order.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Wanda Coleman papers (Collection 2282). Available at UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
Alexandra Garrett papers and Records of the Beyond Baroque Foundation (Collection 1615). Available at UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
S.A. Griffin collection of underground poetry, Scott Wannberg, and The Carma Bums (Collection 2248). Available at UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.

controlaccess.subject: Poets, American -- California -- Los Angeles -- Archives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This box contains 13 poetry notebooks, three CD-Rs, six audiocassettes, and a conceptual puzzle box designed by Harris' grandchild.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: BOX CONTAINS UNPROCESSED DIGITAL and AUDIO MATERIALS: Digital and audio materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box contains poetry notes on legal pads and within notebooks.

did.unittitle: Publications, poetry note cards, and photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box contains personal photographs, poetry on notecards, publications from Harris' library, and reading copies of his work: Against the Day of the Dead, where love is, and 5 by John Harris.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box contains files Against the Day of the Dead through Coming to Terms.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box contains files Coming Down from Tai-Shan through Girl With Guitar.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box contains files Half Life through Leaving L.A.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box contains files LA LA Land through Northwest Forecast.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box contains files Ocean through Surf and Sky.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box contains files Song Without Music through You, Weaver.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box contains files for Dawn, Climbing, and Saltwater among others. Folder 4 includes an Al's Bar flier for a poetry reading with Harris, Bill Mohr, and Bob Flanagan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box contains files for Trust, Climbing, and a Westpoint Inn binder of poetry.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box contains research files for Santa Cruz Island, Santa Monica Mountains, Mountain Restoration Trust, and Indians.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box contains files on the Joy of Walking, mile charts, Wilderness Institute, hiking Big Bear country, Mulholland, trail guides, and cartography.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box contains files on drive-up peaks, roadside archaeology, Navajo, rock art, West Point Inn, Santa Monica, Coastwalk, California State Parks, travel writings, backpacking permits, and First Aid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box contains files on Rancho El Conejo, The Black Oak, Whitman, Jeffers, and the LA literary scene.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box contains fliers, lease agreement, space diagrams, budget sheets, photographs, clippings, Poetry Marathon materials, and submissions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box contains Bachy submissions, Bob Flanagan typescript, mailing list, clippings, correspondence, reviews, Los Angeles Poetry Society materials, photographs, publicity paste-ups, Beyond Baroque materials, programs, maps, notes, and poetry walks/readings materials.

did.unittitle: Poetry workshops and readings files, correspondence, and ephemera

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box contains fan mail to Harris; clippings; reviews; LA poetry scene materials; fliers for readings at Beyond Baroque, dA Center, Sunland-Tujunga Library, Rapp Saloon, Village Books, Midnight Special, Rose Cafe; workshop materials, and ephemera such as an empty BACHY submission envelope dated 1974 July 6 from Wanda Coleman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box contains photographs, clippings, and correspondence in addition to files on suicide survivors, family lineage, China, Hawaii, and disability benefits.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box contains files on biographies, resumes, Sport Chalet, AA, SA, Higher Power Hikes, Higher Power retreats, spiritual retreats, and labyrinths.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This box contains the following publications: The Bridge no. 1; Hanging Loose no. 8; Beyond Baroque 701 vol. 1, no. 3; Southern California Lit Scene vol. 1, no. 1; Invisible City no. 2, 4, 5, 8; Venice Thirteen no. 2; Westways 1971 and 1972 July; Beyond Baroque 712 vol. 2, no. 2; Three issue 3; BEAR no. 2; The Venice Beachhead no. 34; Big Boulevard no. 6, vol. 1; Beyond Baroque 731 vol. 3, no. 2; BACHY vol. 11, no. 1; Venice Poetry Workshop VPW 5; The L.A. Voice vol. 1, no. 5; Poetry Flash 29; Los Angeles Herald Examiner 1983 July 31; Reader's Guide to Los Angeles 1983 September 30; and Palisades 90272 vol. 5, no. 9.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This box contains an issue of Pace magazine (1969 February) and the following broadsides: 8 x 8 New Poems in Broadside Format (1975), A Canon for Francis Crick (1980), Climbing (1999), and Katrina Song (2008).
